title: Burn scar review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports thermal, scald, electrical, chemical, friction, depth, blistering, redness, pain, site, and mechanism documentation.
assessment.rationale: The UAE DHA burns guideline supports documenting the original burn mechanism, location, timing, depth or extent already assessed, and relevant history; it does not provide a complete mature-scar follow-up standard.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns
source.population: Adults and children with thermal, scald, chemical, electrical, friction, or inhalational burn presentations.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth burn documentation and escalation; definitive treatment remains clinician-led and serious burns require in-person or emergency assessment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports thermal, scald, electrical, chemical, friction, depth, blistering, redness, pain, site, and mechanism documentation.

assessment.text: Supports escalation for face or neck and airway involvement, inhalation, chemical or electrical burns, circumferential or deep burns, large surface area, special sites, comorbidity, infection, or associated trauma.
assessment.rationale: The DHA red-flag section supports recording assessed severe pain, infection or systemic concern, and clinician escalation decisions without asserting findings or generating referral.
